questions.question: Explain the role and responsibilities of a Database Administrator (DBA).
keyPoints: Installing and configuring database software
Managing user accounts and security privileges
Performing regular backups and implementing recovery strategies
Monitoring performance and optimizing queries
Ensuring data integrity and enforcing constraints
Managing database storage and capacity planning
Implementing disaster recovery procedures

questions.question: What are the four ACID properties of database transactions? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Atomicity: All operations complete successfully or none do
Consistency: Database moves from one valid state to another
Isolation: Concurrent transactions appear to execute serially
Durability: Committed changes persist even after system failure

questions.question: Differentiate between incremental and differential backups with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Incremental backup stores only data changed since last backup (full or incremental)
Differential backup stores changes since last full backup
Incremental uses less storage but longer restore time
Differential is faster to restore but uses more storage
Example: Day1 Full(100GB), Day2 Inc(10GB), Day3 Inc(5GB) vs Day2 Diff(15GB), Day3 Diff(20GB)

questions.question: Explain the concept of database security. Discuss various types of privileges.
keyPoints: Database security protects data from unauthorized access
System privileges: CREATE SESSION, CREATE TABLE, CREATE USER, etc.
Object privileges: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on tables/views
GRANT and REVOKE commands manage privileges
Role-based access control groups privileges into roles for easier management

questions.question: What is a deadlock? How can it be prevented in database systems?
keyPoints: Deadlock occurs when two or more transactions wait indefinitely for each other to release locks
Prevention techniques: Wait-Die, Wound-Wait, Timeout mechanism
Detection: Wait-for graph analysis
Resolution: Victim selection and rollback
Prevention is better than detection for most systems

questions.question: Explain the database recovery process after a system failure.
keyPoints: Analysis phase: Identify transactions to be rolled back/redone
Redo phase: Reapply all committed transactions from log
Undo phase: Rollback uncommitted transactions using log
Use of checkpoints to minimize recovery time
Transaction log maintains before and after images of changes

questions.question: Discuss the importance of database normalization and list the first three normal forms.
keyPoints: Normalization eliminates data redundancy and improves data integrity
1NF: No repeating groups, atomic values only
2NF: 1NF + No partial dependencies (non-key attributes depend on entire key)
3NF: 2NF + No transitive dependencies (non-key attributes depend only on key)
Higher normal forms reduce anomalies in insert, update, delete operations

questions.question: Explain the concept of transaction management in DBMS. How does the system ensure the ACID properties during concurrent transaction execution?
keyPoints: Transaction is a logical unit of work with ACID properties
Concurrency control ensures isolation in concurrent environments
Lock-based protocols: Shared locks (S-lock) and Exclusive locks (X-lock)
Two-phase locking: Growing phase (acquire locks) and Shrinking phase (release locks)
Timestamp-based ordering: Assign unique timestamps to transactions
Multiversion concurrency control (MVCC) allows multiple versions of data
Deadlock detection and prevention mechanisms
Checkpoints and transaction log for durability and atomicity

questions.question: A company wants to implement a comprehensive backup and recovery strategy for their critical database. Discuss the various backup types, recovery models, and best practices they should adopt.
keyPoints: Backup Types: Full, Incremental, Differential, Online, Offline, Logical, Physical
Recovery Models: Full Recovery, Bulk-logged Recovery, Simple Recovery
Backup Strategy: Weekly full + daily differentials + hourly transaction log backups
Recovery Procedures: Restore full backup, then differential, then transaction logs
Test recovery plan regularly
RTO and RPO definitions based on business requirements
Disaster recovery site considerations
Automation of backup verification

questions.question: Describe the database security architecture. Explain how authentication, authorization, and auditing work together to protect database resources.
keyPoints: Authentication: Verifying user identity (password, certificate, biometric)
Authorization: Determining permitted actions after authentication
Privileges: System and object level permissions
Roles: Grouping privileges for easier management
Auditing: Tracking user activities and database changes
Authentication methods: OS authentication, password authentication, certificate-based
Authorization models: DAC, MAC, RBAC
Security best practices: Least privilege, separation of duties, regular audits

questions.question: Discuss the various performance tuning techniques that a DBA can employ to optimize database performance.
keyPoints: Query optimization: Use EXPLAIN plans, create appropriate indexes
Indexing strategies: B-tree, bitmap, covering indexes
Memory management: Buffer pool sizing, query cache
I/O optimization: Disk layout, RAID configuration
Statistics maintenance: Update table and index statistics
Connection pooling: Reduce connection overhead
Proactive monitoring: Set performance baselines and alerts
Partitioning: Horizontal and vertical partitioning for large tables
Materialized views: Pre-compute expensive queries
